notes: Removable flash memory card format. 256MB SD card held 4 hours of compressed video in 2003. Became dominant removable storage format evolving through SDHC/SDXC/SDUC with terabyte capacities. Related entities: hand-held-entertainment, panasonic.
Sony proprietary removable flash memory format. 1GB cost $500 in 2003. Sony eventually phased out Memory Stick in favor of SD cards as industry standardized. Related entities: sony.

Direct methanol fuel cell for portable electronics. NEC demonstrated laptop prototype with 5-hour battery life in 2003. Consumer fuel cell laptops never materialized; found niche use in backup power and military. Related entities: nec.
High definition television standard. In 2003 HDTV adoption was still early. Roku HD1000 designed for HDTV screens. Became mainstream by late 2000s; superseded by 4K/8K. Related entities: roku.
Wireless LAN technology. Roku HD1000 included WiFi for networked content access. Became ubiquitous in consumer electronics. Related entities: roku, hand-held-entertainment.
Linux-based OS for embedded consumer electronics. Roku HD1000 ran Linux with developer SDK for third-party apps. Became foundation for Android and numerous IoT/CE devices. Related entities: roku.

Video compression standard. Hand Held Entertainment provided MPEG-4 conversion software for ZVUE player. MPEG-4 and successor H.264/AVC became dominant streaming codecs. Related entities: hand-held-entertainment.

SD-based multimedia storage for pre-loaded licensed content on SD cards. Early attempt at physical media distribution via flash memory; superseded by downloads and streaming. Related entities: hand-held-entertainment.

Digital video recorder technology pioneered by ReplayTV (Anthony Wood). Competed with TiVo. DVR functionality eventually absorbed into cable boxes and streaming platforms. Related entities: replaytv, roku.
